story: Senior swagger vanished the night I met Annie—faded jeans, a bohemian blouse, a ruby smile. We shared burgers, a starry blanket, and a hush that felt like forever. In Riverview Park, amid daffodils, I vowed, “My heart is yours to hold.” She promised to keep it. That morning an early-enrollment letter had invited Annie to start college on the coast; she circled a departure date six weeks away in her planner. The next evening I headed back to our wrought-iron bench to talk about summer and the fall to come.
implausible_knowledge: That morning an early-enrollment letter had invited Annie to start college on the coast; she circled a departure date six weeks away in her planner.
question: When John meets Annie at the bench to make plans for the coming months, what will he most likely suggest?
answer_with_knowledge: Discuss her imminent move and ask to plan a long-distance relationship for after she leaves.
answer_without_knowledge: Propose local plans—like a late-August camping trip and enrolling together at the nearby college.
